briefdescription.para: This function is the main entry point for the interrupt example using the XDpTxSs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function will set up the system with interrupts and set up Hot-Plug-Event (HPD) handlers.

parameterdescription.para: is the unique device ID of the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the system setup failed.
XST_SUCCESS should never return since this function, if setup was successful, is blocking.

simplesect.para: If system setup was successful, this function is blocking in order to illustrate interrupt handling taking place for HPD events.

briefdescription.para: This function initialize required platform-specifc peripherals.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if required peripherals are initialized and configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function setup stream source to input DisplayPort TX Subsystem.

parameterdescription.para:
specifies whether to split video frame vertically into two different vertical halves.
1 = Vertically split input frame
0 = No vertically split input frame.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if stream source is configured successfully.

briefdescription.para: This function sets up the interrupt system so interrupts can occur for the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

detaileddescription.para: The function is application-specific since the actual system may or may not have an interrupt controller. The DPTX Subsystem core could be directly connected to a processor without an interrupt controller. The user should modify this function to fit the application.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if interrupt setup was successful.
A specific error code defined in "xstatus.h" if an error occurs.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) event is received by the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpTxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for HPD event.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when a Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) pulse is received by the DisplayPort TX Subsystem core.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpTxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for HPD pulse.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when the lane count change interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpTxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for lane count change.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when the link rate change interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpTxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for link rate change.

briefdescription.para: This function is called when the pre-emphasis and voltage swing adjustment interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpTxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler for pre-emphasis and voltage swing adjust.

briefdescription.para: This callback is called when RX MSA values to be copied into TX MSA.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

simplesect.para: Use the XDpTxSs_SetCallback driver function to set this function as the handler MSA copy.

briefdescription.para: This is a common function to set up DisplayPort TX Subsystem for Hot-Plug-Event (HPD).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XDpTxSs instance.

specifies stream transport mode from the following
1 = Multi Stream Transport
0 = Single Stream Transport

briefdescription.para: This is the main function for XDpTxSs interrupt example.

detaileddescription.para: If the DpTxSs_IntrExample function which sets up the system succeeds, this function will wait for the interrupts. Once a connection event or pulse is detected, DpTxSs will RX device capabilities and re-start the subsystem.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if the interrupt example was unsuccessful.

simplesect.para: Unless setup failed, main will never return since DpTxSs_IntrExample is blocking (it is waiting on interrupts for Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD) events.

briefdescription.para: This file contains a design example using the XDpTxSs driver in single stream (SST) transport or multi-stream transport (MST) mode with interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: Upon Hot-Plug-Detect (HPD - DisplayPort cable is plugged/unplugged or the monitor is turned on/off), DisplayPort TX Subsystem will read the capability of RX device and re-start the subsystem.

simplesect.para: This example requires an interrupt controller connected to the processor and the DisplayPort TX Subsystem HIP in the system. For this example to display output, the user need to implement initialization of the system (DpTxSs_PlatformInit) and after DisplayPort TX subsystem start (XDpTxSs_Start) is complete, implement configuration of the video stream source in order to provide the DisplayPort TX Subsystem HIP input. The functions DpTxSs_PlatformInit and DpTxSs_StreamSrc are declared and are left up to the user implement.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00 sha 07/01/15 Initial release. 2.00 sha 09/28/15 Added HDCP, Timer Counter interrupt handier registration. Added set MSA callback. 4.1 ms 01/23/17 Modified xil_printf statement in main function to ensure that "Successfully ran" and "Failed" strings are available in all examples. This is a fix for CR-965028.
